patterns_recognized: D-grade picks where BOTH SP-edge-against AND PQI-against simultaneously oppose the model pick remain directionally unreliable — confirmed 0-for-4 on 5/20 (PIT@STL, TEX@COL, OAK@LAA, CHW@SEA) and 0-for-2 on 5/18 (OAK@LAA, LAD@SD). Applied as DOWNGRADE on CLE@DET today; directional caution noted for PIT@STL and OAK@LAA.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD/PLATINUM prevention) fires on ATL@MIA (−6.01pp), TOR@NYY (−3.42pp), COL@ARI (−15.89pp). All three correctly zero stake. Market pricing superiority over F-signal stacks has been validated 6+ times in archive; do not override on F-signal conviction alone.

BvP context adds nuance without flipping picks: ATL's Acuña Jr. (44 PA, OWNER vs Alcantara, shrunk 0.902) and Albies (48 PA, OWNER, shrunk 0.891) provide meaningful qualitative support for ATL direction despite no-stake ruling. Same-matchup recency pattern (TOR@NYY 5/20: TOR won 2-1 with NYY PLATINUM + HARD CAP 1 identical structure) is a qualitative caution signal for TOR@NYY today, but HARD CAP 1 already manages the exposure; f5_full_delta=0.1228 is below the 0.15 mandatory DOWNGRADE threshold. CONFIRM B- stands.
